popularStory.description:
Halifax County - Will Book O p.310 
WILL OF WILLIAM ECHOLS - Dated: 8 Apr 1771; Prob: 16 May 1771

Lend to Sarah Echols [Sarah TURNER], my wife, the land I now live on plus my corn mill & all my movea …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hannah Echols於大約1734年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州時，她的父親William Echols31歲，母親Sarah Turner30歲。她於大約1758年與Vardry McBee在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州哈利法可安特里姆 帕里什結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1823年在美國肯塔基州享年90歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hannah Echols于大约1734年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州时，她的父亲William Echols31岁，母亲Sarah Turner30岁。她于大约1758年与Vardry McBee在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州哈利法可安特里姆 帕里什结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1823年在美国肯塔基州享年90岁。

nameMeanings.name: Echols
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of English   Eccles   and possibly also of German   Eckels  .

variants.name: Eccles
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Eccles in Lancashire, or possibly from any of several other places so named in Kent, Norfolk, Berwickshire, and Dumfriesshire, from British eglēs ‘Romano-British Christian church’ (from Latin ecclesia, Greek ekklēsia ‘gathering, assembly’). Such places would have been the sites of notable pre-Anglo-Saxon churches or Christian places of communal worship.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: "Patrick Henry made his ""Give me Liberty or Give me Death"" speech in Richmond Virginia."
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Becomes the 15th State
historicEvents.description: On June 1, 1792, Kentucky became the 15th state. It was the first state west of the Appalachian Mountains
Lewis and Clark set out from St. Louis, MO to explore the West.

historicEvents.title: Kentucky Bend Created
historicEvents.description: During the New Madrid earthquakes of 1811-1812, the Kentucky Bend or New Madrid Bend was created. It is located in the southwestern corner of Kentucky on the banks of the Mississippi River.
historicEvents.urlTitle: RootsRated-The Fascinating Story of Kentucky Bend: A Barely-Inhabited Isolated Piece of the State Cut-Off by a Curve in the Mississippi River

en.lifeSummary: When Hannah Echols was born about 1734, in Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, William Echols, was 31 and her mother, Sarah Turner, was 30. She married Vardry McBee about 1758, in Antrim Parish, Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1823, in Kentucky, United States, at the age of 90.

Elle a épousé Vardry McBee environ 1758, à Antrim Parish, Halifax, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1823, à Kentucky, États-Unis, à l’âge de 90 ans.

ja.Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial Amer: イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ハリファックス
ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Echolsが約1734年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州で生まれた時，父William Echolsは31歳，母Sarah Turnerは30歳であった。約1758年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ハリファックスアントリム パーイッシュでVardry McBeeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1823年にアメリカ合衆国ケンタッキー州で90歳で死去した。

ko.Birch Creek, Halifax, Virginia, United S: 미국 버지니아 주 할이파스 비르치 크리크
ko.Antrim Parish, Halifax, Virginia, Britis: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 할이파스 앤트림 파리시
ko.Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial Amer: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 할이파스
ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Echols(이)가 대략 1734년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Echols(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Turner(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1758년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 할이파스 앤트림 파리시에서 Vardry McBee(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1823년 미국 켄터키 주에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hannah Echols nasceu aproximadamente 1734, em Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, William Echols, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Turner, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Vardry McBee aproximadamente 1758, em Antrim Parish, Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1823, em Kentucky, Estados Unidos da América, com 90 anos.

ru.Spartanburg, Spartanburg, South Carolina: спартанбург, спартанбург, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты

ru.Thicketty, Cherokee, South Carolina, Uni: тиккетти, чироки, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hannah Echols родилась около 1734 г. в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени William Echols было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Sarah Turner, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Vardry McBee около 1758 г. в местности антрим пэриш, хэлифакс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1823 г. в местности Кентукки, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 90 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hannah Echols nació aproximadamente 1734, en Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, William Echols, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Sarah Turner, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Vardry McBee aproximadamente 1758, en Antrim Parish, Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1823, en Kentucky, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 90 años.
